statements.impact_statement: False CPE match. liquibase-commercial-mongodb is a Liquibase extension, not MongoDB server. The vulnerability applies to MongoDB server only.
False CPE match. liquibase-license-utility is not liquibase-core. The vulnerability only affects liquibase-core versions < 4.8.0.

GraalVM Python setuptools ReDoS vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to parse HTML during package lookup; no package lookups occur since all packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin.

Stack exhaustion DoS in json-smart via unlimited recursive parsing of deeply nested JSON arrays and objects. Requires parsing attacker-controlled untrusted JSON input. Liquibase is a CLI tool that only processes JSON from local operator-controlled configuration files and database metadata. No network-facing JSON parsing surface exists.
Fixed in azure-identity 1.10.0. Liquibase Secure uses azure-identity 1.18.2, which includes the fix.

urllib3 cookie header leak vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. No HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.
Fixed in azure-identity 1.12.2. Liquibase Secure uses azure-identity 1.18.2, which includes the fix.
Fixed in msal4j 1.15.1. Liquibase Secure uses msal4j 1.23.1, which includes the fix.

sqlparse ReDoS vulnerability. sqlparse is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its SQL parsing functionality is never invoked at runtime. Liquibase uses its own Java SQL parser; the Python sqlparse module is never called.
False CPE match. cassandra-driver-kerberos is not the Authenticator 2FA app. The vulnerability applies to the Authenticator mobile application.

Stack exhaustion DoS in json-smart via deeply nested JSON with many { characters (incomplete fix for CVE-2023-1370). Requires parsing attacker-controlled untrusted JSON input. Liquibase is a CLI tool that only processes JSON from local operator-controlled configuration files and database metadata. No network-facing JSON parsing surface exists.

GraalVM Python setuptools command injection vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to process URLs via download/install functions; those functions are never called at runtime.

Spring Boot EndpointRequest.to() creates a security matcher for null/** instead of the actuator endpoint path when the endpoint is disabled. Requires a running Spring Boot web application with Spring Security and actuator endpoint configuration. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Spring application context, never runs an HTTP server, never exposes actuator endpoints, and never configures Spring Security.

statements.status_notes: Apache Tomcat RCE via Default Servlet partial PUT — file fragments uploaded to predictable temp paths trigger deserialization of assembled content. Also enables reading security-sensitive files.
statements.impact_statement: Remote code execution and information disclosure via Tomcat Default Servlet partial PUT support. Requires an HTTP PUT request to a running Tomcat servlet container with partial PUT enabled. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts the Tomcat HTTP server, never deploys any servlet, and never opens any network port. The Default Servlet, partial PUT handling, and file session persistence code paths are never activated.

Netty SslHandler resource exhaustion vulnerability via SSLEngine. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious SSL/TLS data to a Netty server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run a TLS server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport for outbound connections.

Spring Framework annotation resolution bypass in generic type hierarchies affecting @EnableMethodSecurity. Requires a running Spring application with method-level security annotations on generic type hierarchies. Liquibase is a CLI tool that does not use Spring Security, does not enable @EnableMethodSecurity, and does not start a Spring application context. The spring-core artifact is a transitive dependency.

GraalVM Python setuptools path traversal vulnerability. setuptools is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its package management functions are never invoked at runtime. This CVE requires setuptools to process package inputs; no package installation or processing occurs at runtime.

Denial of service via excessive memory consumption in Tomcat multipart upload processing. Requires sending a crafted multipart/form-data HTTP POST to a running Tomcat instance. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server and never accepts HTTP requests. The multipart parsing code path is completely inactive.

Denial of service via HTTP/2 stream reset memory leak in Tomcat. Requires an HTTP/2 connection to a running Tomcat server. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server, never enables HTTP/2, and accepts no inbound network connections.

PostgreSQL JDBC driver channel binding bypass. The vulnerability only triggers when the JDBC connection property channelBinding is explicitly set to "require" (default is "prefer"). Liquibase does not configure channelBinding=require in any default configuration. The vulnerable enforcement code path in ConnectionFactoryImpl.doAuthentication() is not reachable under Liquibase's default configuration.

Denial of service via integer overflow in Tomcat multipart upload file size limit validation. Requires sending a crafted multipart HTTP upload to a running Tomcat server. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server and never processes inbound HTTP requests or multipart uploads.

Denial of service via uncontrolled resource consumption in Tomcat HTTP/2 stream management. Requires an HTTP/2 client connecting to a running Tomcat server. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server, never enables HTTP/2, and never binds a network port.

Netty HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame validation bypass in codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/2 frames to a Netty HTTP/2 server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP/2 server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport.

Path traversal and potential RCE via regression in Tomcat Rewrite Valve URL normalization. Requires an HTTP request to a running Tomcat server with the Rewrite Valve configured. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server, never configures the Rewrite Valve, and never accepts HTTP requests.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

False positive due to classifier confusion. Trivy reads the JAR's internal pom.properties which reports version 13.2.1 (without the jre11 classifier), causing a false version comparison. The installed JAR is 13.2.1.jre11 which contains the fix. Microsoft's non-standard Maven versioning uses a classifier suffix instead of a standard version bump.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

urllib3 vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. All Python packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin; no HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.

urllib3 vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. All Python packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin; no HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

GnuPG vulnerability in OS packages. GnuPG is installed in the Docker image for package signature verification during build but is not invoked at runtime by Liquibase. The Liquibase application does not call any GnuPG binaries.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package (localization). GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.
GnuPG binary package. GnuPG is installed for build-time package signature verification only; not invoked at runtime by Liquibase.

urllib3 vulnerability. urllib3 is bundled as a transitive dependency within GraalVM Python extensions but its HTTP client functionality is never invoked at runtime. All Python packages are pre-installed at build time via Maven graalpy-maven-plugin; no HTTP requests are made via Python at runtime.

Incomplete OCSP verification in Tomcat's FFM/OpenSSL TLS integration. Requires an active TLS listener with client certificate authentication and OCSP validation configured. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server or binds any TLS connector. tomcat-embed-core is a transitive dependency from spring-boot-starter-web; the TLS and OCSP subsystems are never initialized.

HTTP request/response smuggling via invalid chunk extension in Tomcat HTTP/1.1 parser. Requires an active HTTP listener processing inbound requests. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server. tomcat-embed-core is a transitive dependency from spring-boot-starter-web; the HTTP parser is never invoked.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

statements.status_notes: Jackson Core 3.x maxNestingDepth constraint bypass in multiple parser implementations, enabling DoS via StackOverflowError from deeply nested JSON input.
statements.impact_statement: Nesting depth constraint bypass in Jackson Core 3.x UTF8DataInputJsonParser and ReaderBasedJsonParser. maxNestingDepth from StreamReadConstraints is not enforced, allowing a deeply nested JSON document to cause StackOverflowError. Liquibase is a CLI tool that only parses JSON from trusted, operator-controlled configuration files and changelog artifacts. It does not expose any JSON parsing endpoint to remote or untrusted input. As established in the prior assessment of GHSA-2m67-wjpj-xhg9, Liquibase does not rely on StreamReadConstraints as a security boundary.

TLS 1.3 cipher suite preference order not preserved in Tomcat's SSL connector configuration. Requires an active TLS connector performing cipher negotiation. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server or binds a TLS connector. tomcat-embed-core is a transitive dependency from spring-boot-starter-web; no TLS handshakes occur.

statements.status_notes: Apache Tomcat authentication bypass where OCSP validation soft-fails even when soft-fail is explicitly disabled, allowing revoked client certificates to authenticate.
statements.impact_statement: Authentication bypass due to CLIENT_CERT soft fail misconfiguration in Tomcat's OCSP validation. Requires an active HTTPS connector configured with CLIENT_CERT authentication and an OCSP responder. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that uses picocli for command-line parsing and never starts a Spring application context or Tomcat server. tomcat-embed-core is a transitive dependency from spring-boot-starter-web, pulled in by the Spring Boot integration module for users who embed Liquibase in their own Spring apps. No connector, no authenticator, no OCSP — the code is inert.

Information disclosure via padding oracle vulnerability in Tomcat EncryptInterceptor (clustering component). Requires Tomcat clustering with EncryptInterceptor enabled. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server or initializes clustering. tomcat-embed-core is a transitive dependency from spring-boot-starter-web; EncryptInterceptor is never instantiated.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

Go stdlib vulnerability in the Docker base image. Liquibase is a Java application and does not execute Go code at runtime. The Go binary is part of Docker base image tooling and is not invoked by Liquibase.

statements.status_notes: Netty HttpObjectDecoder incorrectly terminates chunk header parsing at \r\n inside quoted strings in chunked transfer encoding extensions, enabling HTTP request smuggling.
statements.impact_statement: HTTP request smuggling via chunked transfer encoding in Netty HTTP codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/1.1 requests to a Netty HTTP server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport for outbound connections. There is no listening socket for an attacker to exploit.

statements.status_notes: Netty verifyContinuationFrame fails to limit the number of zero-byte CONTINUATION frames, bypassing maxHeaderListSize and causing excessive CPU consumption on the server.
statements.impact_statement: HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame flood DoS in Netty HTTP/2 codec. This is a server-side vulnerability requiring an attacker to send malicious HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frames to a Netty HTTP/2 server. Liquibase is a database migration CLI tool that does not run an HTTP/2 server. Netty is a transitive dependency from Azure SDK, Cassandra driver, and MongoDB driver — used only as a client-side transport. There is no inbound HTTP/2 listener.

Information disclosure via improper encoding in Tomcat JsonAccessLogValve. Requires an active HTTP connector with JsonAccessLogValve configured and non-default relaxedPathChars/relaxedQueryChars values. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server. tomcat-embed-core is a transitive dependency from spring-boot-starter-web; no access log valves are configured or initialized.

Information disclosure via sensitive data in Tomcat log files from the Kubernetes cloud membership provider (clustering). Requires Tomcat clustering with Kubernetes membership enabled. Liquibase is a CLI tool that never starts a Tomcat server or initializes clustering. tomcat-embed-core is a transitive dependency from spring-boot-starter-web; Kubernetes cluster membership is never initialized.

statements.status_notes: Jackson Core 3.x StreamReadConstraints.maxDocumentLength check can be bypassed in multiple code paths, weakening DoS protection for applications that rely on it.
statements.impact_statement: Document length constraint bypass in Jackson Core 3.x. The StreamReadConstraints.maxDocumentLength check can be bypassed in blocking parsers, async parsers, and DataInput path. jackson-core 3.x is a transitive dependency from Spring Boot 4.0.3 in liquibase-license-utility. Liquibase does not set maxDocumentLength (zero usages in codebase), does not process untrusted JSON input from external sources, and is a CLI tool — not a network-facing service. The constraint bypass only matters if relied upon for DoS protection, which Liquibase does not.

statements.status_notes: CVE-2022-0839 is an XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability in Liquibase Core versions prior to 4.8.0 via the SAXParser. An attacker could exploit this to read arbitrary files or perform server-side request forgery (SSRF) through crafted XML changelog files.
statements.impact_statement: XXE vulnerability fixed in Liquibase 4.8.0. Our liquibase-core is built from source with the fix included. Scanner incorrectly matches the non-semantic version format (master-<sha>).
